title: Add support for orientation for symbols with `_horz/_vert` similar to the `_(right|left|up|down)`
description: Adds logic to ensure schematic symbols with orientation suffixes (_horz_vert) maintain correct visual orientation after group placement rotations.
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure schematic symbols that include an orientation suffix (`_horz`/`_vert`) keep the correct visual orientation after group placement rotations. 

### Description
- Added logic in `lib/components/primitive-components/Group/Group_doInitialSchematicLayoutMatchPack.ts` to detect a symbol name suffix matching `_(horz|vert)`. 
- Compute a normalized rotation from `placement.ccwRotationDegrees` and, for 90° or 270° rotations, replace the suffix to swap `_horz` ↔ `_vert`. 
- Also kept the existing behavior that rotates text positions and direction suffixes like `_(right|left|up|down)`. 
- Updated the generated snapshot `tests/repros/__snapshots__/repro108-multiple-gunn-diode-fails-to-render-schematic.snap.svg` to reflect the adjusted layout/rotation output.

### Testing
- Ran the automated test suite with snapshot tests and updated the failing snapshot at `tests/repros/__snapshots__/repro108-multiple-gunn-diode-fails-to-render-schematic.snap.svg`; the test run succeeded.

title: Support rotated pill-hole-with-rect-pad plated hole
description: Internally converts platedhole shapepill_hole_with_rect_pad pcbRotation...  to rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad, matching the existing rotated hole behavior, and adds reverse circuit-json mapping for compatibility.
body:
- internally convert `<platedhole shape="pill_hole_with_rect_pad" pcbRotation={...} />` to `rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad`, matching the existing rotated `hole` behavior
- add the reverse circuit-json mapping so `rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad` imports back into `PlatedHole` with `pcbRotation`
- keep rotated and non-rotated paths as separate explicit branches for readability
- add a regression test and snapshot for rotated pill-hole-with-rect-pad plated holes

title: Include vias in obstacle connectivity
description: Fixes autorouter obstacle generation so pcb_via obstacles include their own connectivity id instead of always using an empty connectedTo list.
body:
Fixes autorouter obstacle generation so pcb_via obstacles include their own connectivity id instead of always using an empty connectedTo list.

  Previously, vias were emitted as anonymous obstacles, unlike SMTPads and plated holes, which meant routed copper could not recognize them as connected copper in the simple-route obstacle model.

title: Add phased local autorouting by routingPhaseIndex
description: Implements phased local autorouting for groups, allowing higher-priority routes to be preserved by routing traces and nets in phases based on their routingPhaseIndex.
body:
Implements phased local autorouting for groups. Traces and nets are grouped by
  routingPhaseIndex, numbered phases route first, and null routes last. After each
  phase, routed trace geometry is added as obstacles for later phases so higher-priority
  routes are preserved.

title: Deduplicate logical ports from composite footprint copper
description: Fixes composite footprints where multiple copper primitives share the same logical pin, such as an SMT pad plus plated hole with the same portHints.
body:
Fixes composite footprints where multiple copper primitives share the same logical pin,
  such as an SMT pad plus plated hole with the same portHints.

  Previously, footprint port discovery returned one port per copper primitive, so repeated
  pin1/pin2 hints could inflate the inferred pin count and create extra logical pins like
  pin3 and pin4.

  This adds a shared helper to normalize footprint portHints, merge aliases, and dedupe
  ports by logical pin number for both string and JSX footprints.

- Bump `@tscircuit/props` devDependency to `^0.0.506` and apply small test/typing cleanups in `intrinsic-jsx.ts` and component test fixtures.

title: Add DRC error when routed trace is thinner than requested
description: Adds a routing check that reports a pcb_trace_error when a trace is routed thinner than its explicit requested thickness, making the issue visible to users.
body:
Motivation
Right now, if the autorouter cannot honor an explicit trace thickness because of clearance limits, it quietly routes a thinner trace with no feedback. That makes routing results misleading and hard to debug. Showing an error makes the problem visible and helps users understand that their requested thickness was not actually achieved.

This change adds a routing check that reports a pcb_trace_error when a trace is routed thinner than its explicit requested thickness. Instead of silently accepting a narrower routed trace, the checker now surfaces the mismatch as a DRC-style error.

title: Fix ambiguous via selectors from creating broken traces
description: Fixes ambiguous via selectors to prevent invalid trace endpoints in circuit designs.
body:
**Fix:**
This change fixes PrimitiveComponent selector resolution so port only lookups no longer fall back to returning a whole component. That means .via is now treated as an ambiguous multi port selector instead of silently resolving to the Via component and creating invalid trace endpoints.

**Motivation:**
Before this change, .via could be accepted as if it were a valid single port, which led to null port ids and later “trace has no PCB trace” failures. Failing early at selector resolution makes the behavior correct, clearer for users, and consistent with how other multi-port components are handled.

title: Remove redundant circuitJson casts in preview viewers
description: Removes redundant as any casts when passing circuitJson into the preview viewers in CircuitJsonPreview, improving type safety and reducing unnecessary usage of any.
body:
## What changed

Removed redundant `as any` casts when passing `circuitJson` into the preview viewers in `CircuitJsonPreview`.

Updated:
- `PcbViewerWithContainerHeight`
- `CadViewer`
- `CircuitJsonTableViewer`

## Why

`circuitJson` is already typed as `CircuitJson | null` in this component, so these casts were hiding type information instead of helping. Removing them makes the code easier to reason about and preserves stronger type checking at the call sites.

## Impact

- Improves type safety in the preview layer
- Reduces unnecessary `any` usage
- Makes future type regressions easier to catch

## Root cause

These casts were likely added as a shortcut during integration, even though the surrounding component already had enough type information for these props.

## Validation

- Reviewed the local diff
- Ran `./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- No runtime behavior changes intended

title: Add `pcb_via_trace_clearance_error` overlay rendering
description: Adds rendering for pcb_via_trace_clearance_error in the SVG output, allowing users to visually identify clearance errors on PCBs.
body:
### Motivation
- PCB DRC results include a `pcb_via_trace_clearance_error` but the SVG renderer did not draw a visual marker for that error type. 
- Renderers should show DRC overlays (markers + labels) when `shouldDrawErrors` is enabled so users can locate violations on the PCB snapshot. 

### Description
- Add `create-svg-objects-from-pcb-via-trace-clearance-error.ts` to render a red diamond marker and centered label at the error `center`, using `message` or a fallback formatted from `actual_clearance` and `minimum_clearance`.
- Wire the new generator into the PCB SVG conversion by importing it and adding a `case "pcb_via_trace_clearance_error"` in the `createSvgObjects` switch so the error is emitted when present and `shouldDrawErrors` is true.
- Include `pcb_via_trace_clearance_error` in the `TYPE_PRIORITY` ordering so it appears with other overlay/error visuals.
- Add a snapshot test `tests/pcb/pcb-via-trace-clearance-error.test.tsx` and a corresponding snapshot to verify the marker and label appear in the generated SVG.

### Testing
- Ran the new test with `bun test tests/pcb/pcb-via-trace-clearance-error.test.tsx`, which passed and wrote the snapshot successfully.
- Re-ran an existing related test `bun test tests/pcb/pcb-component-outside-board-error.test.tsx`, which also passed.
- All automated test runs completed with successful passes for the modified areas.

title: Fix createPngUrl to generate PNG URLs from svg.tscircuit.com
description: Fixes createPngUrl to generate PNG URLs through svg.tscircuit.com instead of the legacy png.tscircuit.com host.
body:
## Summary

This fixes `createPngUrl` so it generates PNG URLs through `https://svg.tscircuit.com/` instead of the legacy `png.tscircuit.com` host.

## Root Cause

`createPngUrl` was still building URLs against `png.tscircuit.com`, while PNG rendering now works through the SVG service using `format=png`.

## Changes

- route `createPngUrl` through `createSvgUrl(..., { format: "png" })`
- add a regression test that verifies the generated URL uses `svg.tscircuit.com`, preserves the requested view, and includes `format=png`
- document `createPngUrl` usage in the README

## Impact

- keeps PNG URL generation aligned with the current rendering service
- reduces divergence between the public PNG helper and the main SVG URL builder
- prevents regressions where `createPngUrl` points at the wrong host

## Validation

- `bun test`

## Verified URLs

- PCB: `https://svg.tscircuit.com/?svg_type=pcb&code=H4sIAJsBqGcAAy2NTQ7CIBhE9z3FhFW7KlWXhUO4ckuBClF%2BAp%2FRxHh30Xb3JvMyY185FYKxq3rcCf0AIdF3wLwkVQye3pATbOIhMDjrr472JJvUtGKrr5QKNlBR2ybcGNaUKBcfm89P%2FMAQVWjVeWKo2l3E%2B%2FhB1ssG429tHv%2Bfshu%2BlSYxzJYAAAA%3D&format=png`

- Schematic: `https://svg.tscircuit.com/?svg_type=schematic&code=H4sIAJsBqGcAAy2NTQ7CIBhE9z3FhFW7KlWXhUO4ckuBClF%2BAp%2FRxHh30Xb3JvMyY185FYKxq3rcCf0AIdF3wLwkVQye3pATbOIhMDjrr472JJvUtGKrr5QKNlBR2ybcGNaUKBcfm89P%2FMAQVWjVeWKo2l3E%2B%2FhB1ssG429tHv%2Bfshu%2BlSYxzJYAAAA%3D&format=png`

title: Auto-mirror bottom-layer pcb_note_text and add regression coverage
description: Auto-mirrors bottom-layer pcb_note_text when no explicit mirror override is provided, aligning the renderer with the newer circuit-json mirrored-from-top-view field and adding regression tests for coverage.
body:
## Summary
- auto-mirror `pcb_note_text` on the bottom layer when no explicit mirror override is provided
- align the renderer with the newer `circuit-json` mirrored-from-top-view field by updating the dependency
- add a regression test and snapshot covering bottom-layer note text mirroring
- update note-related test fixtures and the panel fixture to satisfy the latest `circuit-json` type requirements

## Why
Bottom-layer `pcb_note_text` was rendering unmirrored, which made note text inconsistent with other bottom-layer text rendering. The root cause was that the note-text renderer never forwarded mirroring information into `drawText`, so bottom-layer notes could not inherit the expected default behavior.

Updating `circuit-json` also surfaced stricter required fields in existing test fixtures, so this branch includes the minimal fixture updates needed to restore type-check cleanliness with the newer schema.

## Impact
This makes bottom-layer PCB note text render from the top-view perspective the same way copper text does, while still allowing explicit mirroring overrides from the circuit JSON model. It also keeps the test suite aligned with the current schema by providing required `layer` and `thickness` fields in affected fixtures.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/elements/pcb-note-text-bottom-auto-mirror.test.ts tests/elements/pcb-note-text-small.test.ts tests/elements/pcb-note-text-custom-color.test.ts tests/elements/pcb-note-text-anchor-alignment.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`

title: Tighten Route Move Semantics to Prevent Over-Translation and Enforce Consistent Trace-Clearance Regression Guards
description: Refines route generation and standardizes trace-clearance limits to enhance move evaluation reliability and prevent clearance regressions.
body:
This PR improves move evaluation reliability by refining how translated routes are generated and by standardizing trace-clearance regression limits during candidate validation.

It removes unnecessary translation expansion for non-translate-only moves, making route adjustments more precise, and reuses the shared trace-clearance regression cap to keep validation behavior consistent across the repair pipeline. The result is safer move generation, more predictable route corrections, and stronger protection against clearance regressions.

title: Add start/end aliases for trace endpoints
description: Adds ergonomic aliases start and end for trace endpoints in addition to existing from and to forms, enhances validation for portRef values, and updates documentation accordingly.
body:
### Motivation
- Allow `start`/`end` as ergonomic aliases for trace endpoints in addition to existing `from`/`to` forms so callers can use either naming style.
- Prevent a runtime error when validating `portRef` values that are non-object by making the custom validator robust to non-object inputs.
- Keep generated docs in sync with source schema changes.

### Description
- Add a third variant to `traceProps` that accepts `start: portRef` and `end: portRef` alongside the existing `path` and `from`/`to` variants in `lib/components/trace.ts`.
- Harden the `portRef` `z.custom(...)` validator to check that the value is a non-null object and that `getPortSelector` is a function before accepting it.
- Add a unit test `tests/trace.test.ts` that verifies parsing of `start`/`end` aliases.
- Regenerate component type documentation (`generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md`) and run code formatting to keep generated artifacts up to date.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/trace.test.ts` and all tests passed.
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` for type checking and it succeeded.
- Ran generation scripts `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts` and they completed successfully.
- Ran `bun run format` to format files successfully.

title: perf(benchmark): disable progress-frame capture in dataset benchmark for faster solve timing
description: Disables intermediate progress-frame capture during solve execution in the dataset benchmark to reduce runtime overhead and improve performance measurements.
body:
## Summary
This PR updates the dataset benchmark runner to disable intermediate progress-frame capture during solve execution.

### Change
- In `scripts/benchmark-datasets.ts`, set:
  - `captureProgressFrames: false` when constructing `HighDensityRepairSolver` in worker runs.

## Why
`captureProgressFrames` is useful for step-by-step visualization/debugging, but it adds significant runtime overhead (extra frame creation/cloning/allocation).  
For benchmark mode, we want to measure solver performance more directly, so this overhead should be excluded.

## Impact
- Faster benchmark execution and lower reported average solve time.
- No change to solver repair behavior or output quality checks.
- Benchmark logs will show fewer frame/iteration progression details tied to captured progress frames.

## Validation
- `bun run typecheck` passes.
- `./benchmark.sh --dataset dataset02` shows improved average solve time compared to runs with progress-frame capture enabled.

title: Eliminate Persistent Boundary-Buffer Violations by Clamping Drifted Routes and Enforcing Trace-Safe, Margin-Aware Cleanup
description: Fixes recurring boundary-repair failures by implementing boundary point clamping and improving margin-aware violation detection, ensuring reliable boundary cleanup without increasing trace-clearance violations.
body:
This PR fixes recurring boundary-repair failures where slightly out-of-bounds or boundary-flush routes remained unresolved.
It introduces boundary point clamping, improves margin-aware violation detection, and adds targeted cleanup that is applied only when it does not increase trace-clearance violations.
Result: more reliable boundary cleanup with stronger safety against trace-quality regressions.

This includes new CSS classes such as `.nav-links-desktop`, `.nav-menu`, `.nav-menu-toggle`, and `.nav-menu-panel` for layout and styling. **Trust-strip Marquee Enhancement:**

* Refactored the trust-strip (company/lab/robotics badges) to use `.trust-strip-track` and `.trust-strip-group` classes, enabling a horizontal marquee animation for desktop and a vertical stack for mobile, with reduced motion support. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R760-R776) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1227-R1382) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R1391) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1113-R1251)
